title: NEC Spends ₹339.06 Crore on Development Projects in Mizoram During 15th Finance Commission Period
content:
117/2026-2027

28th May, 2026:The North Eastern Council (NEC) has continued to strengthen its developmental engagement in Mizoram through major investments in healthcare, education, connectivity, tourism, agriculture, and livelihood sectors during the 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) period.

According to an official press release, Mizoram received a cumulative expenditure of ₹339.06 crore under the Schemes of NEC (SoNEC) and NESIDS-Roads programmes during the period.

Under the SoNEC programme, NEC spent ₹123.83 crore on various developmental initiatives across the state. The projects covered medical and veterinary facilities, market infrastructure, educational institutions, cultural centres, sports infrastructure, and community development works.

Special emphasis was also given to agriculture and allied sectors through promotion of wet rice cultivation, potato seed production, piggery, poultry, fisheries, arecanut cultivation, passion fruit processing, and livestock development.

The NEC further supported preservation and promotion of Mizoram’s cultural and natural heritage through the establishment of cultural heritage centres, tourism circuits, ecological parks, and other cultural infrastructure aimed at boosting eco-tourism and cultural tourism in the state.

During the 15th Finance Commission period, six road projects worth ₹277.99 crore were implemented in Mizoram under the NESIDS-Roads programme, out of which two projects valued at ₹25.19 crore have already been completed.

The Council also highlighted the upgradation of the Saiha-Lungbun-Tlangnuam-Haka Road, which is expected to improve connectivity between Mizoram’s southern districts and the Myanmar border, supporting the broader objectives of India’s Act East Policy.

NEC also expanded its youth and scientific outreach initiatives through programmes such as the Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme and NE-SPARKS during 2025–26.

Under the Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme, 581 students from across the country visited premier institutions in the Northeast, including Mizoram University, which hosted 44 students from Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep in January 2026.

Meanwhile, under NE-SPARKS (North East Students’ Programme for Awareness, Reach and Knowledge on Space), 99 students from Mizoram were among 786 students from the Northeastern states who visited the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) during 2025–26 to promote scientific awareness and innovation.

The NEC also released funds during April 2026 for ongoing projects in Mizoram, including livestock rearing support in the Sinlung Hills Council area, establishment of a State Veterinary Information and Resource Centre, construction of a market building at West Phaileng in Mamit district, and tourism development at Vangchhia in Champhai district under the initiative “Our Heritage, Our Treasure.”

In addition, NEC released ₹20.09 crore under NESIDS (Roads) during April 2026 for ongoing road projects, including the construction of the Pukzing–Silsury Road and Tualpui–Champhai Road in Mizoram.

Attachment

title: 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) hun chhungin NEC-in Mizoram tan cheng vaibelchhe 339.06 a seng
content:
738/2026-2027

28th May, 2026:North Eastern Council (NEC) chuan 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) hun chhungin Mizoram-ah healthcare, zirna, inkalpawhna kawngpui, tourism, agriculture leh mamawh hrang hrangah hmasawnna hnalian tam tak a thawk.

NEC Official press release-in a tarlan danin Schemes of NEC (SoNEC) leh NESIDS-Roads programme hnuaiah Mizoram-ah cheng vaibelchhe 339.06 crore sen a ni.

SoNEC programme hnuaiah hian NEC-in cheng vaibelchhe 123.83 sengin medical leh veterinary, bazar, zirna in, cultural centre, infiamna leh khawtlang mamawh hmasawnna ruhrel pawimawh tak tak sak thar leh thuam that hna a thawk a. Kuthnathawktute tan leilet siam, alu (potato) thar chhuah, vawk leh ar vulh, sangha dil siam, kuhva hmun siam, sapthei sawngbawl leh ranvulh kawnga hmasawnna atan hma nasa takin a la bawk.

Pi leh pute sul hnu, hmun hlui leh Mizo culture humhalhna turin cultural heritage centre, tourism circuit, ecological park leh cultural infrastructure dangte siam a ni a. Heng ruhrel din leh cheithat te hi eco-tourism leh cultural tourism hmasawnna atan pawh tangkai hle tura ngaih a ni.

NESIDS-Roads programme hnuaiah 15th Finance Commission hun chhungin Mizoram-ah cheng vaibelchhe 277.99 man hu road project paruk (6) kalpui a ni a. Chung zinga project pahnih, cheng vaibelchhe 25.19 senna chu zawh fel tawh a ni.

NEC chuan Saiha-Lungbun-Tlangnuam-Haka kawngpui siamthat hna pawh a thawk a. Hei hian Mizoram chhim lam district te leh Myanmar ramri inkara inkalpawhna tha zawk a siam bakah, India Sawrkar Act East Policy tihhlawhtlinna kailawn pawimawh tak a ni.

Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme hnuaiah India ram hmun hrang hrang atangin zirlai 581 te chuan Northeast-a Zirna In pawimawhte an tlawh a, January 2026 khan Andaman & Nicobar Islands leh Lakshadweep atangin zirlai 44 te chuan Mizoram University an tlawh a ni.

NE-SPARKS (North East Students’ Programme for Awareness, Reach and Knowledge on Space) programme hnuaiah North East State hrang hrang atangin zirlai 786 te chuan Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) an tlawh a, an zingah hian Mizoram atangin zirlai 99 (mipa 45 leh hmeichhia 54) an tel an ni.

April 2026-ah khan NEC chuan Sinlung Hills Council area-a khawsak harsa zawk te tan ranvulh tanpuina a pe a, State Veterinary Information and Resource Centre a din a, West Phaileng-ah market building leh Champhai district-a Vangchhia-ah hmasawnna ruhrel din nan sum a pechhuak (release) bawk a ni.

Hei bakah hian NESIDS (Roads) hnuaiah April 2026 khan cheng vaibelchhe 20.09 release a ni a. Hei hi kawngpui sial leh cheithat hna kalpui mek te bakah Pukzing–Silsury kawngpui leh Tualpui–Champhai kawngpui siam hna thawh nana hman tur a ni.

Attachment
